Chapter:
because reading matters.

Chapter gives educators the data they need to understand what students actually comprehend from their readings, and gives students the tools to engage more deeply with the material.

★ ? ▲ ○

I went down yesterday to the Piraeus with Glaucon the son of Ariston, that I might offer up my prayers to the goddess; and also because I wanted to see in what manner they would celebrate the festival, which was a new thing. I was delighted with the procession of the inhabitants; but that of the Thracians was equally, if not more, beautiful. When we had finished our prayers and viewed the spectacle, we turned in the direction of the city; and at that instant Polemarchus...

The servant took hold of me by the cloak behind, and said: Polemarchus desires you to wait. I turned round, and asked him where his master was. There he is, said the youth, coming after you, if you will only wait. Certainly we will, said Glaucon...

May there not be the alternative, I said, that we may persuade you to let us go? But can you persuade us, if we refuse to listen to you? he said. Certainly not, replied Glaucon. Then we are not going to listen; of that you may be assured.

Polemarchus said to me: I perceive, Socrates, that you and your companion are already on your way to the city. You are not far wrong, I said. But do you see, he rejoined, how many we are? Of course. And are you stronger than all these? for if not, you will have to remain where you are.

The Problem

There is a fundamental disconnect between assigned reading and true understanding.

Students often read in isolation, struggling with complex texts without support. Educators assign chapters with no visibility into true comprehension until it's too late. This silence leaves both students and teachers in the dark.

+
+
How It Works

Simple to use.
Serious in depth.

Chapter is a dedicated reading platform built for Educators. Students engage with assigned readings using purpose-built tools. Those interactions become actionable data for educators.

01

Upload & Define Outcomes

Educators upload texts and define the learning objectives they expect students to meet.

02

Students Actively Annotate

Students read and employ reading tools.

03

Actionable Educator Insights

Analytics help educators make more informed decisions.

+
+
The Platform

Built for every stakeholder

Click each feature below to explore a live showcase of our reading environment and analytics dashboards.

Feature 01

Clutter-Free Reader

A focused, clean reading environment designed to minimize cognitive load. Students engage directly with margin tools while distractions are kept out of sight.

Feature 02

Active Annotations

Highlights, margin comments, and student reading milestones are logged in context. These metrics sync silently to populate aggregate datasets.

Feature 03

Educator Analytics

Educators track class progress maps, time-on-page stats, and paragraph highlights. Spot comprehension gaps instantly before class begins.

Page 7 · Book II 18 highlights  ·  12 notes  ·  21 readers

According to the tradition, Gyges was a shepherd in the service of the king of Lydia; there was a great storm, and an earthquake made an opening in the earth at the place where he was feeding his flock. Amazed at the sight, he descended into the opening, where, among other marvels, he beheld a hollow brazen horse, having doors, at which he stooping and looking in saw a dead body of stature, as appeared to him, more than human, and having nothing on but a gold ring; this he took from the finger of the dead and reascended.

Suppose now that there were two such magic rings, and the just put on one of them and the unjust the other; no man can be imagined to be of such an iron nature that he would stand fast in justice. No man would keep his hands off what was not his own when he could safely take what he liked out of the market.

High engagement Medium Light General areas where student highlights and notes cluster

Our Team

Who's building chapter

A small, focused team of engineers and builders working to give educators real insight into student comprehension and students better tools to engage with their readings.

L. B. Harper III
Founder & Manager

Founder of Chapter Reading LLC and graduate of Florida State University. Larry built Chapter around a single conviction: universities deserve real data on how their students learn.

LinkedIn
Will Schmidt
Software Engineer

Will leads front-end and full-stack development, translating product vision into a fast, intuitive reading experience built for academic use.

LinkedIn
Jessica Neelam
Data Engineer

Jessica architects Chapter's tracking and data pipeline, ensuring analytics are robust, accurate, and FERPA-compliant from the ground up.

LinkedIn
Jyothsna Vellanki
Full Stack Developer

Jyothsna leads full-stack development, building robust web applications and seamless user experiences. A graduate of Western Illinois University (WIU), she brings strong technical expertise to Chapter's engineering team.

LinkedIn
Jason Broughton
Advisor

Director at the Library of Congress. Jason brings deep institutional knowledge of libraries, reading access, and academic governance to guide Chapter's strategic growth.

LinkedIn
FAQ

Common Questions

Everything you need to know about integrations, compliance, and how Chapter operates.

What analytics do educators receive?

Educators will have a robust set of analytics that include average reading time, page hotspots (e.g., which pages get the most engagement from students), which concepts and themes students engage the most with and why, and a comprehension accuracy report.

How does Chapter help students?

Chapter provides educators the information necessary to help tailor their efforts toward accommodating students. The goal is to help educators help students.

For what kind of students is Chapter best for?

For Chapter to be successful, it assumes that the student can read. Chapter is not a tool designed to help students to read and develop fluency. Its primary focus to help educators focus their efforts on where students need it most.

When will Chapter be available?

Our team is working daily to ensure that Chapter is available for the year of 2026.

Ready to begin your chapter?

Join the educators and institutions working to close the gap between assigned readings and actual student comprehension.
